Items.Title: 探索 Chaos Genius 方法来削减数据仓库成本并提高投资回报率 | Discover the Chaos Genius Way to Slash Your Data Warehouse Costs and Boost ROI
Items.Description:
大数据和云计算的出现对许多行业产生了深远的影响。 如果您正在考虑为您的企业获得竞争优势，您可能已经考虑过跟踪搜索引擎排名以及其他可以帮助您决定将时间和金钱花在哪里的数据。 您将在有限的预算下工作，因此您将需要每一分钱。

本文探讨了如何使用Chaos Genius优化数据仓库的成本。 对于不同行业的各种公司而言，数据仓库的成本一直在上升——从医疗保健机构、初创公司、开源公司到使用大数据分析工具的小型/大型制造商。 手动成本计算效率低下，但 Chaos Genius 计划通过自动化复杂分析来解决这个问题，以便企业可以更快速、更轻松地获得洞察力。

在本文中，我们研究了监控数据仓库成本的挑战，以及我们如何利用支持 ML 的分析开源工具（如 Chaos Genius）的强大功能来自动化和优化此过程。

与数据仓库相关的成本

数据仓库是任何类型业务中最复杂但最重要的组件之一。 他们拥有所有关键数据，使公司能够对其未来做出决策。 不幸的是，数据仓库的维护和运行也可能非常昂贵。 与数据仓库相关的主要成本包括：

  • 聘请熟练的专业人员进行设计、实施和集成
  • 设置托管数据库服务器的基础设施
  • 构建一个 ETL（提取、转换、加载）过程，可以将事务提要有效地摄取到数据库中
  • 开发应用程序代码来查询这些数据库并生成报告

除此之外，还有其他隐性成本，如维护、支持、升级等，乍一看可能并不明显，但如果计算不当，会随着时间的推移而增加！

数据仓库涉及如此多的移动部件，对于没有该领域专业知识或经验的组织来说，可能很难知道在尝试优化成本时应该从哪里开始。

基于云的数据仓库

基于云的数据仓库提供了许多好处，但也并非没有挑战。 云成本管理和优化是许多企业的障碍。 您可以使用电子表格或其他工具来跟踪您的成本，但这些方法不可靠，并可能导致报告不准确或不一致。

有多种类型的数据仓库，它们具有许多不同的成本。 这可能会导致难以将一个数据仓库与另一个数据仓库进行比较，甚至难以了解您自己企业的数据仓库成本是多少。

手动成本优化

优化数据仓库成本的第一步是了解您当前的成本是多少。 这将为您提供一个基准，以便在您对基础架构进行更改时进行比较。 为此，您需要了解每个数据仓库正在使用何种资源（例如，存储、CPU 时间）以及这些资源每月的总成本。 您可以通过查看来自您的云提供商或他们提供的其他工具的报告来做到这一点，这些工具允许您查看每项服务每小时使用了多少容量。

一旦您了解了当前的成本是多少，您就可以确定哪些服务推高了成本。

手动成本管理的问题

手动数据仓库成本管理面临的问题包括：

  • 大量时间消耗：需要手动收集和记录来自不同部门的数据，非常耗时。 它还需要付出大量努力，以确保跟踪项目不同阶段和方面产生的成本的准确性和一致性。
  • 无法扩展：随着公司规模的增长，手动方法无法很好地扩展。 随着您的业务增长并变得更加复杂，跟踪所有费用变得更加困难。
  • 容易出错且不可靠的估算：手动跟踪成本可能会导致直到项目结束甚至完成后才能检测到的错误。 这些错误可能导致不正确的报告，这会在根据不准确的信息做出财务决策时造成问题。
  • 缺乏透明度：人工跟踪通常是在闭门造车的情况下进行的，让利益相关者对项目的资金使用情况一无所知。 这使得他们不太可能批准未来的资金请求或质疑管理层做出的支出决定。

它有助于监控和分析大量业务、数据和系统指标。 它提供对数据的多维深入分析和洞察、检测异常、启用智能警报、执行季节性分析、自动检测根本原因 - 以及更多功能！

它为任何企业个人提供了追踪成本所需的所有信息——如下面的屏幕截图所示，您可以看到 KPI 摘要部分提供了有关上个月和当月指标的详细信息，计算了这些指标的变化，并提供了在此期间检测到的异常情况。

向下钻取部分提供了基于特定服务器区域的所有指标。

此外，您还可以获得无限的指标和社区支持。 它还提供专业服务，除了用户访问管理支持外，您还可以获得安全和合规支持。

除了业务可观察性，Chaos Genius 还提供了雪花可观察性，有助于优化和监控雪花的成本，同时提高雪花查询的性能。

通过分析您的 Snowflake 查询、数据库和资源使用情况，Chaos Genius 使您能够更好地提高这些系统的效率，并将支出减少 10% 到 30%。

价格非常实惠，分为三层。 第一层是免费的，另外两层是面向业务的计划，适用于希望监控更多指标的公司。

结论

对基于云的解决方案的需求猛增。 随着每天生成大量数据，数据仓库已成为任何商业智能或分析平台不可或缺的一部分。 这就是 Chaos Genius 的用武之地。自动根本原因分析和异常检测只是使用该产品的众多好处中的两个。Chaos Genius 利用 AI 和 ML 的力量立即提供有关最佳策略和路线修正的建议。 更重要的是，它有可能增加企业的利润，因为它可以节省数据仓库成本，同时确保遵守法规。



原文:

The advent of big data and cloud computing has had far-reaching effects across many industries. If you're thinking of getting a competitive edge for your business, you've probably already considered tracking search engine rankings as well as other data that can help you decide where to spend time and money. You'll be working with a limited budget, so you'll need every penny you can get.

Chaos Genius lets its users extract, integrate, analyze, and visualize data from the web. This article explores how to optimize the cost of a data warehouse with Chaos Genius. The cost of data warehouses has been on the rise for a variety of companies in different industries—from healthcare agencies, startups, open source companies,  to small/big manufacturers using big data analytics tools. Manual cost calculations are inefficient, but Chaos Genius plans to solve this problem by automating complex analysis so that businesses can get insights more quickly and easily.

In this article, we examine the challenges in monitoring data warehouse cost and how we can automate and optimize this process by utilizing the power of ML-enabled analytics open source tools like Chaos Genius.

Costs associated with Data Warehouses

Data warehouses are one of the most complex yet vital components to any kind of business. They hold all the critical data that allows companies to make decisions about their future. Unfortunately, data warehouses can also be very expensive to maintain and run. The major costs associated with a data warehouse include:

  • Hiring skilled professionals for design, implementation, and integration
  • Setting up the infrastructure for hosting the database server(s)
  • Building an ETL (extract, transform, load) process that can ingest transactional feeds efficiently into the database(s)
  • Developing application code to query these databases and generate reports

In addition to all this, there are also other hidden costs such as maintenance, support, upgrades, etc., which may not be obvious at first glance but add up over time if not accounted for properly!

With so many moving parts involved with a data warehouse, it can be difficult for an organization without specialized knowledge or experience in this area to know where they should begin when trying to optimize costs.

Cloud-Based Data Warehouses

Cloud based data warehouses offer many benefits, but it's not without its challenges. Cloud cost management and optimization are an obstacle for many businesses.  You can use spreadsheets or other tools to track your costs, but these methods are unreliable and can lead to inaccurate or inconsistent reporting.

There are various types of data warehouses and they come with many different costs. This can make it difficult to compare one data warehouse to another or even know what your own business’s data warehouse costs are.

Manual Cost Optimization

The first step in optimizing data warehouse costs is understanding what your current costs are. This will give you a baseline to compare against as you make changes to your infrastructure. To do this, you'll need to get a sense of what kind of resources are being used by each data warehouse (e.g., storage, CPU time) and how much those resources are costing in total each month. You can do this by looking at reports from your cloud provider or other tools they provide that allow you to see how much capacity is being used by each service on an hourly basis.

Once you have a sense of what your current costs are, you can identify which services are driving up the cost.

Issues with manual cost management

The issues faced with manual data warehouse cost management include the following:

  • Massive time consumption: It is time-consuming as it involves manually collecting and recording data from different departments. It also requires a lot of effort to ensure accuracy and consistency in tracking the costs incurred during different stages and aspects of the project.
  • Does not scale: A manual approach does not scale well as the size of your company grows. As your business grows and becomes more complex, it becomes harder to keep track of all your expenses.
  • Error-prone and unreliable estimates: Manually tracking costs can lead to errors that may not be detected until the end of the project or even after its completion. These errors could result in incorrect reporting, which would cause problems when making financial decisions based on inaccurate information.
  • Lack of transparency: Manual tracking usually takes place behind closed doors, leaving stakeholders in the dark about how funds are being spent on a project. This makes them less likely to approve future funding requests or question spending decisions made by management.

Chaos Genius: An Effective Tool to Analyze Data Warehouse Costs

Chaos Genius is a fully open source machine learning-powered analytics engine for outlier detection and root cause analysis. It helps in monitoring and analyzing high-volume business, data, and system metrics. It provides multidimensional drill-downs and insights into your data, detects anomalies, enables smart alerting, performs seasonality analysis, automates root cause detection—and a lot more features!

Chaos Genius employs machine learning and artificial intelligence (ML/AI) to sift through data and provide more precise cost projections and enhanced metrics for the data warehouse, allowing businesses to save time and effort while making more informed decisions.

It provides all the information necessary for any business individual to track down their costs—as shown in the screenshot below, you can see the KPI summary section gives details about the previous and current month’s metrics, calculates the changes in those metrics, and provides the anomalies it detected during that period.

The drill-down section provides all the metrics based on a particular server region.

Chaos Genius is completely open source, and you can use it free of charge. In addition, you get unlimited metrics and community support. It also offers a pro service, where you can get security and compliance support in addition to user access management support.

Aside from business observability, Chaos Genius also provides Snowflake Observability, which helps optimize and monitor the cost of a Snowflake while also improving the performance of snowflake queries.

By analyzing your Snowflake queries, databases, and resource usage, Chaos Genius enables you to better enhance the efficiency of these systems and reduce expenditures by 10% to 30%.

The pricing is quite affordable, with three tiers. The first tier is free, and the other two are business-oriented plans intended for companies that want to monitor more metrics.

Conclusion

The demand for cloud-based solutions has skyrocketed. With the massive amounts of data being generated every day, data warehouses have become an integral part of any business intelligence or analytics platform. This is where Chaos Genius comes in. Automated root-cause analysis and Anomaly Detection are just two of the many benefits of using this product.. Chaos Genius harnesses the power of AI and ML to immediately supply advice on optimal strategies and course corrections. More importantly, it has the potential to increase a business's margins as it saves on data warehouse costs while ensuring compliance with regulations.

Items.Title: 关于在科技领域创造成功职业的 16 条建议 | 16 Suggestions on Creating a Successful Career in Tech
Items.Description:
发表于这些是针对有抱负和早期职业开发人员的 16 条可行的职业技巧，以及一些一般性建议以增加背景信息。 它们基于我对我在技术社区中关于创造成功的技术职业的对话的反思。

为有抱负的开发人员提供的 10 个可行技巧

  1. 网络就像你的事业取决于它。 您可以成为最好的编码员，但如果没人知道，那么您的才能几乎一文不值。 目标是通过帮助他人和寻找在团队中工作的机会来让您的才华受到关注。 首先在 LinkedIn 上创建个人资料并与周围的人建立联系。 接下来，本着与同龄人建立联系的想法，努力参加在线和实体活动。

  2. 每天抽出时间编码或学习。 保持一致、负责并继续巩固所学知识的一个好方法是每天提交到 GitHub，在博客上写下你的旅程，或者用标签 #100daysofcode 发推文。

  3. 深入了解技术社区和文化。 这可以通过播客、博客、书籍、聚会等方式实现。如果你想成为一名职业篮球运动员，你可以和其他球员一起出去玩，观看体育频道等。将同样的方法应用于科技行业。

  4. 构建和部署个人项目，而不是只做教程。 目标是使用您计划在工作中使用的工具，并从过程中遇到的问题中学习。 通过完成、打包、部署项目以及对他人产生影响，您将学到比任何教程都多的知识。 吸取的教训将在您的整个职业生涯中使用。

  5. 记录您的学习之旅。 一种类型的文档是您过去克服的问题的调试日志。 没有什么比一次又一次地犯同样的错误更糟糕的了。 为个人项目创建您自己的软件开发生命周期以优化流程。 写下个人学习技能路线图。 最后，与他人分享您的旅程。

  6. 了解您当地的就业市场，并在此基础上确定您想要遵循的技术（前端、后端等）路径。 在掌握框架之前，先学习编程语言的基础知识。

  7. 确保简历上的项目是为了在面试中进行良好的对话而设置的。 与我分享的一个绝妙建议是，在你的简历中只包含你可以广泛谈论的项目（工作或个人）。 您希望能够谈论斗争、如何解决问题以及使用的技术。

  8. 有意识地寻找机会产生影响并使用技术。 对我来说，这就是参加黑客马拉松比赛并在非营利组织做志愿者以获得这种体验。 一个例子是公民黑客马拉松，您可以在其中为当地城市建造一些可能影响生活或节省资金的东西。

  9. 准备面试与面试一样重要。 模拟面试应该是你的生计。 我特别向朋友、家人和我的技术社区询问软推销，然后逐渐提出更难的问题。 针对准备在面试中讨论的几个谈话要点（经验和项目）制定策略。 最后，回顾过去的面试，了解如何做得更好、提出的问题以及需要学习的技能。

  10. 沟通是一种不可思议的天赋。 观察出色的演讲者并模仿他们的口头和非口头交流方式。 找机会谈论技术，例如进行技术讲座、小班授课以及在技术聚会上与他人交谈。

  1. 专注于成为问题解决者，而不仅仅是编码员。 成为问题解决者是一种心态。 初级开发人员专注于根据指示完成工单，中级开发人员专注于需要完成的工作，高级开发人员专注于帮助公司实现目标。

  2. 练习阅读和理解其他人的代码。 大多数开发人员使用其他人编写的代码，并编写其他人最终必须阅读的代码。 这引出了编写好的文档的下一个技巧。

  3. 有意识地全面了解软件/产品的制作方式，而不仅仅是您的贡献。 努力通过所有技术和非技术文档（路线图、用户研究等）了解您支持的产品。 参与有助于塑造产品的每个过程、计划会议、头脑风暴等。

  4. 反馈对于增长至关重要。 这不仅仅是接受反馈，而是积极寻求同事和老板的反馈。 乐于分享你是新手和学习者。 最重要的是，要保持尊重，并考虑到你周围的每个人都知道一些你不知道的事情。

  5. 当您在工作中取得成就、项目和责任时，请更新您的简历。

  6. 做别人不想做的事情。 这将是一座陡峭的山坡，但它会让你变得有价值，并帮助你成为学习者，让你的职业生涯更上一层楼。

从一个部落到一起旅行，指导导师，以及您追随的开拓者； 你是你社交圈的全部。 如果你想在技术领域取得成功，你需要身边有专注于技术的人。

给自己找一个部落。 在我任教的技术新兵训练营中，积极与他人相处的学生通常走得更远。 他们拥有可以激发想法、帮助他们保持积极性并与之共享资源的人。 好处是无穷无尽的。

在技​​术领域，“Take advantage of what is accessible instead of what you don't have”意思是使用可用资源，直到你有机会做更多事情。 你不认识大公司的创始人，但你可以在 LinkedIn 上与开发人员建立联系。 您没有大学实习的机会，因此请参加在线编程马拉松。 你不是在一家初创公司，下一个最好的事情就是为非营利组织做志愿者。 如果没有人愿意雇用你作为自由职业者，那就为家人和朋友开发小产品。

我的结局想法

成功不是一条直线。 你要做的不仅仅是忙碌，不仅仅是遵守规则，最重要的是，在你的职业生涯中要有创新精神。 没有直线路径，而是一系列横向向前、向后、在天花板上和上山的台阶。 没有通往顶峰的电梯，而是需要学习的阶梯。 但你可以做到。

以下是来自社区的关于磨练你的手艺和职业的引述，我已经学会了热爱。

但是当你在泥泞中时，你的腿会变得更强壮，你正在学习勇气，当你到达另一边时，你会变得更好。 所以当你身处其中时，拥抱吸吮。”

我想用这句话结束这场关于创造成功职业的谈话，“永远不要让你的职业成为巧合”。

原文:

JC Smiley

Posted on Dec 18

These are 16 actionable career tips for aspiring and early career developers plus some general advice to add context. They are based on my reflections of conversations I’ve had in my tech community on creating a successful tech career.

10 Actionable tips for aspiring developers

  1. Network like your career depends on it. You can be the best coder but if no one knows it then your talent is almost worthless. The goal is to get noticed for your talents by helping others and finding opportunities to work in a team. Start with creating a profile on LinkedIn and connecting with those around you. Next, strive to attend online and physical events with the idea of connecting with your peers.

  2. Carve out time daily to code or learn. A great way to stay consistent, accountable, and continue reinforcing what you have learned is to commit to GitHub daily, blog about your journey, or tweet with the hashtag #100daysofcode.

  3. Dive into the tech community and culture. This can be done via podcasts, blogs, books, meetups, etc. If you want to be a pro basketball player, you hang out with other players, watch sports channels, etc. Apply that same method to the tech industry.

  4. Build and deploy personal projects instead of only doing tutorials. The goal is to work with the tools you plan to use on the job and to learn from problems you will face along the way. You will learn more from finishing, packaging, deploying a project, and having an impact on others than any tutorial can teach you. The lessons learned will be used throughout your career.

  5. Document your learning journey. One type of documentation is a debugging log of past issues you have overcome. Nothing is worse than falling for the same error again and again. Create your own software development life cycle for personal projects to refine the process. Write down a personal roadmap of skills to learn. Finally, share your journey with others.

  6. Learn your local job market and based on that, determine what path in tech (Frontend, Backend, etc.) you want to follow. Start with learning the fundamentals of a programming language before mastering a framework.

  7. Ensure that the projects on your resume are set up for a good conversation in the interview. A wonderful tip shared with me was to only include projects (work or personal) on your resume that you can speak extensively about. You want to be able to talk about the struggle, how you solved the problem, and the technology used.

  8. Be intentional about seeking opportunities to have impact and working with technology. For me, this was competing in hackathons and volunteering with nonprofits to get that experience. An example is a civic hackathon where you build something for your local city that may impact lives or save money.

  9. Preparation for an interview is just as important as the interview. Mock interviews should be your bread and butter. I specifically ask friends, family, and my tech community for soft pitches and gradually build up to harder questions. Strategize on a few talking points (experiences & projects) ready to be discussed in an interview. Finally, reflect on past interviews for how you can do better, questions that were asked, and skills to learn.

  10. Communication is an incredible talent. Watch people who are great speakers and emulate their verbal and non-verbal communications styles. Find opportunities to talk about tech like giving tech talks, teaching small classes, and talking to others at tech meetups.

  1. Focus on being a problem solver, not just a coder. Being a problem solver is a mindset. Junior developers focus on completing tickets with directions, mid level developers focus on what needs to be done, and senior developers focus on helping the company achieve a goal.

  2. Practice reading and understanding other people’s code. Most developers work on code written by others and write code that others have to read eventually. This leads to the next tip of writing good documentation.

  3. Be intentional about gaining a big picture understanding of how software/products are made, not just your contribution. Strive to understand the product you support via all tech and non-tech documentation (roadmaps, user research, etc.). Get involved in every process, planning meeting, brainstorm, etc. that helps shape the product.

  4. Feedback is critical for growth. It’s not just being receptive to feedback, but actively seeking feedback from colleagues and your boss. Be open to sharing that you are new and learning. Most importantly, be respectful and consider that everyone around you knows something you don’t.

  5. Update your resume as you incur achievements, projects, and responsibilities at work.

  6. Do the things that nobody else is wanting to do. It’s going to be a steep hill to climb, but it will make you valuable and help you to become the learner that allows you to advance your career.

The quote “Success is not a solo sport” reinforces the importance of the people around you. From a tribe to journey with, mentors for guidance, and trail blazers who you follow; you are the totality of your social circle. If you want to be successful in tech, you need tech focused people around you.

Find yourself a tribe. At the tech boot camp I taught at, the students that actively surrounded themselves with others generally went further. They have people they can bounce ideas off of, help them stay motivated, and to share resources with. The benefits are endless.

In tech the saying “Take advantage of what is accessible instead of what you don’t have” means to use the resources available until you have the opportunity to do more. You don’t know the founder of a big company but you can network with the developers on LinkedIn. You don’t have the opportunity for college internships so compete in online hackathons. You aren’t in a startup, the next best thing is to volunteer for a non-profit. If no one will hire you as a freelancer, then build small products for family and friends.

My ending thoughts

Success isn't a straight line. You have to do more than just hustling, more than play by the rules, and most importantly, be innovative in your approach to your career. There is no linear path but a series of steps that transverse forward, backward, on the ceiling, and up mountains. There is no elevator to the top but a staircase of lessons to be learned. But you can do it.

The following is a quote from the community about honing your craft and career that I have learned to love.

“Sometimes you will feel like you are trudging through the mud, and you are making little progress. But while you're in the mud, your legs are getting stronger, you are learning about grit, and when you make it to the other side, you'll be better for it. So while you're in it, embrace the suck.”

I want to end this conversation about creating a successful career with this quote, “Never let your career be a coincidence”.

这些只是我个人的观点，拥有超过 8 年的手动和自动化测试经验。

什么是手动测试？

手动测试是一种软件测试，测试人员在不使用任何自动化工具的情况下手动执行测试用例。 手动测试是所有测试类型中最原始的，有助于发现软件系统中的错误。 任何新应用程序都必须先进行手动测试，然后才能对其进行自动化测试

Items.Title: Solidity 简介 | Introduction to Solidity
Items.Description:
一开始，有比特币。 这是一种以去中心化方式进行货币兑换的快速、简便的方式——无需银行参与。 人们可以信任它，因为每笔交易都记录在无法更改的公共分类账中。

但是比特币是有限的。 如果拥有正确的代码，区块链技术有可能比点对点货币交易做得更多。 因此创建了以太坊网络，并以一种名为 Solidity 的新语言为基础。

什么是坚固性？

比特币为运行交易提供奖励，而以太坊则对执行软件指令收取费用。 以太坊中的 gas 机制让用户可以使用以太币（以太币）为他们想要在 EVM 上执行的指令预付费用。 使用 EVM 运行的合约是使用 Solidity 编写的。

历史观点

  • 作为一种高级语言意味着开发人员无需操纵系统级信息（如内存或字节码）即可创建智能合约来执行计算。

    从 C++ 的 Solidity 的深刻影响可以在变量声明的语法、for 循环、重载函数的概念、隐式和显式类型转换以及许多其他细节中看出。

    在该语言的早期，Solidity 曾部分受到 JavaScript 的影响。 这是由于变量的函数级作用域和关键字 var 的使用。 现在，与 JavaScript 的主要相似之处在于函数是使用关键字 function 定义的。 除了这些要点之外，Solidity 看起来像大多数其他花括号语言，并且不再受 JavaScript 的主要影响。

    对 Solidity 的另一个影响是 Python。 添加了 Solidity 的修饰符，试图用更受限制的功能对 Python 的装饰器进行建模。 此外，多重继承、C3 线性化和 super 关键字以及值和引用类型的一般赋值和复制语义都取自 Python。

    与 Solidity 语言相关的术语

    在使用 solidity 语言进行编程时，存在与球体相关的术语。

    以太坊

    它为近 260,00 种不同的加密货币提供平台。

    以太

    以太币是由以太坊矿工生成的加密货币，用于奖励为保护区块链而执行的计算。

    以太坊虚拟机 (EVM)

    以太坊虚拟机 (EVM) 是用于在以太坊中执行智能合约的运行时环境。 它广泛专注于使用公共节点的国际网络提供安全性和不受信任的代码的执行。 智能合约

    智能合约是高级程序代码，被编译为 EVM 字节码并部署到以太坊区块链以供进一步执行。 它使我们能够在不受第三方干扰的情况下进行可信的交易。 这些交易是可追溯且不可逆转的。

    它是为实现智能合约而设计的高级编程语言。

  • 它是一种静态类型的面向对象（面向契约）的语言。

  • 它受 C++、Python 和 JavaScript 的影响，设计用于在以太坊虚拟机 (EVM) 上运行。

  • 它支持复杂的用户自定义编程库和继承。

  • 它是区块链运行平台的主要语言。

  • 它是一种可用于创建合约的语言，如投票、盲目拍卖、众筹、多重签名钱包等。

以太坊生态系统的美妙之处在于，许多不同的加密货币和去中心化应用程序都可以使用它。 智能合约使得在以太坊上为各种企业和组织开发独特的技术成为可能。

使用 Solidity 构建的智能合约可以被认为是一种在不同人之间自动化业务和非业务流程的方法。 这确保了在区块链上进行交易的人们不必担心欺诈或无法使用相同货币等风险。

使 Solidity 代码的执行成为可能的关键组件之一是 EVM。 在底层，Solidity 创建了在 EVM 上执行的机器级代码。 编译器用于分解人类可读的高级代码，并将其转化为处理器读取的指令。 不同的平台提供免费的 Solidity 编译，包括 Remix 在线编译器和在 PC 上下载的类命令编译器。

其中最重要的问题之一是对用于解析 JSON 结构或浮点运算的有用库函数的访问受限。

为什么选择 Solidity 语言

以太坊区块链的 Solidity 编程的可行性在很大程度上取决于它提供的价值优势。

除了基本功能之外，Solidity 还提供了许多有趣的功能，这些功能证明它是比许多基于以太坊的编程语言更好的选择。

  • 此外，Solidity 编程语言还提供应用程序二进制接口或 ABI 以实现数据类型安全。 当编译器识别出特定变量的任何数据类型不匹配时，应用程序二进制接口 (ABI) 可以帮助快速识别错误。

  • 以太坊中 Solidity 编程的另一个流行亮点指向“自然语言规范”的效果。 使用 Solidity 构建去中心化应用程序和智能合约的其他关键优势包括：

    • 轻松访问智能合约中面向对象的属性，例如多级继承特征。
    • 更简单的语法有助于初学者学习智能合约和区块链开发的基础知识。
    • 在 Solidity 编程语言上开发智能合约将有助于为处理两方协议的各种平台推动安全可靠的流程。

    没有 Solidity = 没有以太坊。 多亏了语言，这就是您可以做的。

    • 一种思考方式就像您在智能手机上获得的应用程序。 您可以从应用商店下载各种应用，从游戏到健身再到银行业务。 主要区别在于它们是开源的并且没有中间人（因此称为“去中心化”）。 就像 Apple 应用商店中的应用程序是为在 iOS 上运行而构建的一样，以太坊上的 Dapps 是为在 Solidity 上运行而构建的。

    • 智能合约：在 Dapps 中，有一些代码被称为智能合约。 这些可以帮助人们在满足特定条件时交换金钱、股票、财产和几乎任何有价值的东西。 这消除了对像公证人这样昂贵的第三方的需要。

    • 这些代币被称为 ERC20 代币； 示例包括 OmiseGO、0x 和 Basic Attention Token。

    结论

    以太坊正在改变。 在以太坊 2.0 的旗帜下，大量的开发和升级捆绑在一起。 这将包括新的股权证明和分片等开发，以使区块链运行得更快。

    这一切都是作为一个开源项目由一大批开发人员和研究人员完成的。 这个社区远远超出了以太坊基金会——以太坊背后的组织。 因此，在此过程中存在问题、分歧和障碍，您可以在我们对以太坊未来的持续报道中阅读其中的一些问题。 但无论发生什么，Solidity 都会存在。

    进一步阅读与资源

    参考

    卡尔文 Ebun A.，（2021 年）。 什么是 Solidity 以及它如何用于开发智能合约？。 马特·赫西 (2019)。 什么是 Solidity 编程语言？ 在Twitter 上关注更多信息。



原文:

In the beginning, there was Bitcoin. It was a fast, easy way to exchange money in a decentralized way – no banks involved. And people could trust it because every transaction is recorded in a public ledger that can’t be changed.

But Bitcoin was limited. Blockchain technology had the potential to do so much more than peer to peer currency exchanges if it had the right code. So the Ethereum network was created, underpinned by a new language called Solidity.

What is Solidity?

Solidity is a brand-new programming language created by the Ethereum which is the second-largest market of cryptocurrency by capitalization, released in the year 2015 led by Christian Reitwiessner.

Solidity is an object-oriented, high-level langauge designed for implementing smart contracts on the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM).

While Bitcoin provides rewards for running a transaction, Ethereum charges fees for executing software instructions. The gas mechanism in Ethereum lets users pre-pay for the instructions they want to execute on the EVM using Ether, its native currency. The contracts that are run using the EVM are written using Solidity.

Solidity is currently the core language on Ethereum and other private blockchains operating on competing platforms, such as Monax and its Hyperledger Burrow blockchain which uses Tendermint for consensus.

Historical View

  • August 2014: The Solidity language is proposed by Gavin Wood.
  • October 2014: Solidity is adopted as a language by Monax, a rival platform.
  • August 2015: Solidity is officially released.

The Solidity project team was led by Christian Reitwiessner, and they were successful in creating a Turing-complete, object oriented programming language for implementing smart contracts. Being a high-level language means developers create smart contracts without having to manipulate system-level information like memory or bytecode to perform computations.

The profound influence of Solidity from C++ can be seen in the syntax for variable declarations, for loops, the concept of overloading functions, implicit and explicit type conversions and many other details.

In the early days of the language, Solidity used to be partly influenced by JavaScript. This was due to function-level scoping of variables and the use of the keyword var. The JavaScript influence was reduced starting from version 0.4.0. Now, the main remaining similarity to JavaScript is that functions are defined using the keyword function. Solidity also supports import syntax and semantics that are similar to those available in JavaScript. Besides those points, Solidity looks like most other curly-bracket languages and has no major JavaScript influence anymore.

Another influence to Solidity was Python. Solidity’s modifiers were added trying to model Python’s decorators with a much more restricted functionality. Furthermore, multiple inheritance, C3 linearization, and the super keyword are taken from Python as well as the general assignment and copy semantics of value and reference types.

Terms Associated with Solidity language

In programming with the solidity language there are terms associated with the sphere.

Ethereum

Ethereum is a decentralized open-source platform based on blockchain domain, used to run smart contracts i.e. applications that execute the program exactly as it was programmed without the possibility of any fraud, interference from a third party, censorship or downime. It serves a platform for nearly 260,00 different cryptocurrencies.

Ehter

Ether is the cryptocurrency generated by ethereum miners used to reward for the computations performed to secure the blockchain.

Ehtereum Virtual Machine (EVM)

Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is a runtime environment for executing smart contracts in ethereum. It focueses widely on providing security and execution of ntrusted code using an internaitonal network of public node. EVM is specialized oto prevent Denial-of-Service attack and confirms that theprogram does not ensure that the commnication is established without any potential interference.

Smart contract

Smart contract are high-level program codes that are compiled to EVM byte codes and deployed to the ethereum blickchain for further execution. It allows us to perform credible transactions without any interference of the third party. These transactions are traceable and irreversible.

Features of Solidity

The features of Solidity programming language can be numerous, and these include:

  • It is high level programming langauge designed for implementing smart contracts.

  • It is a statically typed object-oriented (contract-oriented) language.

  • It is influenced by C++, Python and JavaScript and it is designed to run on the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM).

  • It supports complex user-defined programming libraries and inheritance.

  • It is the primary language for blockchains running platforms.

  • It is a language that can be used to creating contracts like voting, blind auctions, crowdfunding, multi-signature wallet, etc.

How Solidity works

The beauty of the Ethereum ecosystem is that so many different cryptocurrencies and decentralized applications can use it. Smart contracts make it possible for unique technologies to be made on Ethereum for all kinds of businesses and organizations.

Smart contracts built using Solidity can be thought of as a way to automate business and non-business processes between different people. This ensures that people making transactions on the blockchain do not have to worry about risks such as fraud or not being able to use the same currency.

One of the key components that makes the execution of Solidity code possible is the EVM. The EVM is described as a virtual computer on the blockchain which turns people’s ideas into code that runs applications on the blockchain.

Under the hood, Solidity creates machine-level code that is executed on the EVM. A compiler is used to break down high-level human-readable code, which it turns into instructions that the processor reads. Different platforms provide free Solidity compilation, including the Remix online compiler and a downloaded command-like compiler on a PC.

EVM smart contracts have some limitations which need to be addressed. One of the most significant of these is the limited access to useful library functions for parsing JSON structures or floating-point arithmetic.

Why Solidity language

The feasibility of Solidity programming for Ethereum blockchain depends a lot on the value advantages it delivers.

Other than the basic functionalities, Solidity offers many interesting features which verify that it is a better option than many Ethereum-based programming languages.

  • The first benefit of Solidity programming refers to the support for complicated data types and member variables alongside the fundamental data types.

  • Furthermore, Solidity programming language also offers an Application Binary Interface or ABI for enabling data type safety. The Application Binary Interface (ABI) could help in quick identification of errors when the compiler recognizes any data type mismatch for specific variables.

  • Another popular highlight of Solidity programming in Ethereum points towards the effect of ‘Natural Language Specification.’ Solidity leverages the Natural Language Specification for transforming user-centric specifications into a language that is comprehensible to machines.

The other critical advantages of using Solidity to build decentralized applications and smart contracts would include the following:

  • Easy access to object-oriented attributes in smart contracts, such as multi-level inheritance traits.
  • Solidity supports multiple variants of supporting roles by leveraging the Application Binary Interface or ABI.
  • The easier syntax helps beginners in learning the fundamentals of smart contracts and blockchain development.
  • Development of smart contracts on Solidity programming language would help in driving secure and trustworthy processes for various platforms dealing with agreements between two parties.

Applications of Solidity

Solidity is the foundation of the Ethereum network. No Solidity = no Ethereum. Here’s what you can do, thanks to the language.

  • Dapps: The programming language gives developers the ability to create their own decentralized apps. A way to think about is like apps you get on your smartphone. You can download all kinds of apps from the app store, from games to fitness to banking. Dapps are the same. The main difference is that they are open source and don’t have a middleman (hence the word decentralized’). Just like apps in the Apple app store are built to run on iOS, Dapps on Ethereum are built to run on Solidity.

  • Smart contracts: Within Dapps, there are pieces of code known as smart contracts. These help people exchange money, shares, property and nearly anything of value when certain conditions are met. That eliminates the need for an expensive third party like a notary.

  • ICOs: Developers can also create their own tokens and launch an ICO right on the Ethereum platform. These tokens are known as ERC20 tokens; examples include OmiseGO, 0x, and the Basic Attention Token.

Conclusion

Ethereum is changing. The slew of developments and upgrades are bundled together under the banner, Ethereum 2.0. This will include developments like a new proof of stake, and sharding to make the blockchain run faster.

It’s all being done as an open source project by a large group of developers and researchers. This community goes far beyond the Ethereum Foundation – the group behind Ethereum. As such, there have been problems, disagreements, and roadblocks along the way, you can read about some of those issues in our ongoing coverage on the future of Ethereum. But whatever happens Solidity is here to stay.

Futher Reading & Resources

References

Calvin Ebun A., (2021). What Is Solidity and How Is It Used to Develop Smart Contracts?. https://www.makeuseof.com/what-is-solidity/

Matt Hursey, (2019). What is Solidity Programming Language?. https://decrypt.co/resources/solidity. https://www.makeuseof.com/what-is-solidity/

Vedant Chainani, @envoy_. (2022). History and Origin of Solidity. https://dev.to/envoy_/history-and-origin-of-solidity-2mhl

Items.Title: 初学者的前端路线图 | Front-End Roadmap for beginners
Items.Description:
大家好，前端技术堆栈一直是热门话题或写博客。 我从事前端开发超过 8 个月。 在大量库和框架的旅程中，我看到了很多关于这个的流行语。 今天我想谈谈前端入门以及您应该学习哪些技术。

那么，让我们开始吧……

我将这篇文章分为三个部分；

  • 作为 Web 开发人员，我们需要了解一些基本和核心概念才能更好地理解它。

    1. 互联网基础
    2. 服务器-客户端架构

    您可以将其与人体骨骼或车身联系起来。

    它用于设计网页，使它们对用户有吸引力和方便。

    它用于为网站添加更多交互性并使其更具功能性。

    在对 HTML、CSS、Javascript 达到一定程度的信心和掌握之后。 现在是探索库和框架以更少的努力构建美观且可扩展的 Web 应用程序的时候了。

    与庞大的开发人员社区一起使用 Angular 和 Vue 等顶级框架做出反应。

    使用 React 的主要本质是可重用的组件和应用程序的速度。

    它具有基于 JavaScript 的排版、表单、按钮、导航和其他界面组件的设计模板。

    注意：还有其他类似的引导程序替代方案，例如 Tailwind CSS，它是实用程序优先的框架。 围绕这些进行了很多开发辩论。

    这个星球上的每个开发人员都应该知道 git 和 GitHub 来维护他们的代码并在上面进行协作。 这是我开始使用 git 和 GitHub的简要指南

    这可以让您更加确信被测应用程序正在按预期工作。

    它提供了简单而完整的 React DOM 测试实用程序，鼓励良好的测试实践。

    它将模块放置到位以便 Node 可以找到它们，并智能地管理依赖冲突。

    还

    它具有与现有工作流程相同的功能集，同时运行速度更快、更安全、更可靠。

    在本文中，我们提出了您可以遵循的前端 Web 开发路线图。 我们讨论了网络维护和代码测试的基础知识。 我希望这些信息对您有所启发。 另外请注意，本文中最重要的技术是 React.js。 所以我建议你试一试，一旦我确定你永远不会回头。

    让我知道您对这篇文章的看法。 我试图以最简单的方式解释。 我希望你喜欢它！

    此外，我们将不胜感激任何建议或反馈🙏。

原文:

Hello Folks, Front-end tech stack has always been a hot topic talk or to write a blog on. I'm in front-end development for more than 8 months. I have seen lots of buzzwords around this along the journey coz of tons of libraries & frameworks. Today I would like to put my thoughts around getting started with front-end & what tech's you should learn.

so, let's begin...

I have divided this article into three sections ;

  • A: The core of Web
  • B: The UI & Ecosystem
  • C: Code maintenance & Testing

As a web dev, we need to understand some of the basic and core concepts to understand it better.

  1. Internet Fundamentals
  2. HTTP/HTTPS/DNS
  3. Server-Client architecture

HTML is a markup language that tells the browser the layout of the website. You can relate this to the human bone skeleton or car body.

CSS stands for cascading style sheets. It is used to design web pages to make them appealing and handy to the user.

JavaScript is the most popular and the only programming language for front-end web development. It used to add more interactivity to the website and make it more functional.

After reaching a certain level of confidence & mastery in HTML,CSS,Javascript . Now is the time to explore the libraries & framework to build beautiful & Scalable web applications in fewer efforts.

React.js is one of the most popular UI libraries from Facebook & now it's Open Source.

Being with a huge community of developers react stand with top frameworks like Angular & Vue.

The main essence of using react is Reusable components & Speed of application.

Bootstrap is a component-based framework, which means it comes with prebuilt components and includes other utilities for layering displays. It has JavaScript-based design templates for typography, forms, buttons, navigation, and other interface components.

Note: There are other similar alternatives to bootstrap such as Tailwind CSS which is the utility-first framework. There have been a lot of dev debates going around these.

Every developer on this planet should be aware of git & GitHub to maintain their code & collaborate on it. Here is my brief guide for getting started with git & GitHub

Cypress is the new standard in front-end testing that every developer and QA engineer needs.

Cypress can be used to write several different types of tests. This can provide even more confidence that your application under test is working as intended.

The React Testing Library is a very lightweight solution for testing React components.

It Provides Simple and complete React DOM testing utilities that encourage good testing practices.

npm is the package manager for the Node JavaScript platform. It puts modules in place so that Node can find them, and manages dependency conflicts intelligently.

Also

Yarn is a new package manager that replaces the existing workflow for the npm client or other package managers while remaining compatible with the npm registry. It has the same feature set as existing workflows while operating faster, more securely, and more reliably.

In this article, we went throw the roadmap that you can follow for Front End Web Development. We talked about the basics of the web to maintenance & code testing. I hope this information was insightful to you. Also, note that the most important technology in this article was React.js. So ill recommend you to give it a try once I'm sure you'll never look back.

< Happy Coding >

Let me know your thoughts on this article. I tried to explain in the most simple manner. I hope you liked it!

Also, any suggestion or feedback is always appreciated🙏.

  • Twitter

Items.Title: 适应忍耐 | Adapting to Endure
Items.Description:
天然气和石油价格高企，供应链因战争、地缘政治事务和长达 2 年的大流行病而中断。 美国和大多数国家多年来一直在印钞票，并降低贷款利率以促进支出，从而促进经济发展。 他们通过这样做将当前的挑战和问题推迟到以后。

现在阻止通货膨胀的唯一方法就是停止购买，如果我们花的钱少了，经济就会萎缩。 美联储在这方面发挥了重要作用，现在他们也在做同样的事情，其他政府也在关闭水龙头以抽出多余的资金，从而导致资本市场大幅复苏。

经济复苏

由于所有这些的连锁效应，所有经济体和资本市场都在经历巨大的复苏。 增长是由 FOMO 而不是基本面驱动的。 科技股正在下跌，加密货币也是如此。 即使基本面和资产负债表良好，它们也在下降。 我们正在经历 20 年来第三大纳斯达克跌幅 (-28%)。

复苏将持续多久？ 没有人知道，但根据红杉的说法，这不会那么快，需要更长的时间，多长时间？ 比预期的要长。

现在是暂停和重新评估的时候了”——红杉

资本是免费的

到目前为止，资本是免费的，但现在却很昂贵，这意味着投资者和风险投资公司疯狂地向初创公司投钱。 这也是近几年“Startup”成为流行语的原因。 他们在玩估值游戏，这意味着他们根据估值为一家初创公司提供资金，而不是在乎他们赚了多少钱（利润）。 一些印度初创企业的客户获取成本超过 500 元，这意味着他们在营销、销售、广告、推荐计划等方面花费了大量资金。

这对初创公司来说非常好，因为他们不需要考虑还剩下多少跑道。 原因是他们知道他们将在下一轮基于估值的融资中获得更多资金。

注意：跑道是您在用完钱之前可以亏本经营的时间。 如果你每个月要烧掉 10,000 美元，而银行里有 60,000 美元，那么你有六个月的时间才会陷入严重的麻烦。

公司号召性用语

是时候改变。 专注很重要。 不惜一切代价实现增长不再有回报。 不惜一切代价获得高速增长奖励的时代即将结束- 红杉

如果不显示结果（利润），公司将无法获得资金。 这意味着唯一的生存方式是他们需要开始通过改变优先级和降低烧钱率来赚钱。 现在每一块钱都比以前更珍贵了； 你需要开始考虑跑道并相应地导航，否则你将需要解雇人员并最终死亡或以你应得的估值被收购。

最快的生存，

降低烧钱率 → 延长跑道 → 找出成为盈利企业的方法 → 产生现金

这不会很快发生，但迈向中长期有纪律的货币增长的步骤将得到回报，并转化为有意义的升值。

好消息是聪明的公司开始适应；

我们必须学会在约束下工作，不惜一切代价专注于盈利。 我们必须熬过冬天，”Unacademy 的联合创始人兼首席执行官 Gaurav Munjal 在给员工的一封信中说。

他们将减少其子公司 relevel 和 Graphy 的现金消耗，因为它们尚未盈利并且仍在进行分配。 他们为员工规定了截止日期，要求他们在平台上的所有 tets prep 类别中盈利。

什么人才？

如果您不相信他们的愿景和创始人，请离开无利可图的组织（如果您相信，那就另当别论了）

所有工程师的薪水，尤其是开发人员的薪水将随着估值下降的速度而下降。 如果没有多少现金可以花，谁会给你那么多。 最糟糕的是你不能拍卖、谈判或讨价还价（如果适合这里的话），因为每个人都在经历同样的事情。 整个经济将显示出变化，而不是特定的一组公司。

如果你是公司的高成本之一，那么即使你是最聪明或最有才华的人，你也可能会遇到麻烦，这就是经济运作的方式。 这并不意味着你的价值下降了，而是当他们有钱时你得到的报酬更早。

正如我所说，如果你聪明并且适应迅速，你会想出一些办法，你将永远在竞技场中生存。

什么是胜利？

如果拥有所有资金的一方获胜，那么 Zappos 如何与亚马逊抗衡并赢得鞋类产品？ 为战斗做好最充分准备的人将获胜。 会所没有准备好，休息吧。

为了准备，Sequoia 谈到了清单；

面对现实，了解市场情况，不要活在希望中。 控制你的恐惧：如果你在艰难时期表现出勇气，危机就会带来机遇。

你无法在晴天超过 15 辆车，但在下雨时你可以。” 组织中有两种类型的人，传教士和雇佣兵。 传教士是那些相信你的使命的人，而雇佣兵只是为了完成任务，他们不关心你的愿景和使命。 保持核心团队在一起。 对他们对所有事情都保持完全透明，分享你还剩多少跑道，并讨论我们如何利用现有资源做到最好。 作为创始人，你的职责是不要让传教士成为雇佣兵。

确保公司现金流顺畅，减少额外支出。 停止实验性活动并专注于现在有效的方法。 如果需要，即使它很昂贵，也可以借债 coz paisa to chahiye na yar！

根据红杉资本的说法，下一步是什么？

目的不是要成为阴郁的灯塔。 远非如此，我们相信你们中最优秀、最雄心勃勃、最有决心的人将利用这一刻挺身而出，打造真正非凡的东西。 但我们也相信，未来几年的胜利将取决于做出艰难、果断的选择——面对过去两年自由资本的繁荣和扭曲可能掩盖的令人不安的挑战。

本次会议的主要目标是转变我们的集体思维方式。 我们正处于一个不确定和变化的时刻——一个严峻的时刻，您的决定将对贵公司的结果产生重大影响。

感谢大家阅读这篇文章。 我只是在学习以文章的形式在互联网上分享我的想法。 让我知道您的反馈，我将非常感激。

您也可以在其他社交媒体上与我联系： https ://linktr.ee/prathameshdukare

想看我的作品： https ://peerlist.io/prathamesh

原文:

Gas and oil prices are high, and the supply chain is broken due to wars, geopolitical affairs and a 2-year long pandemic. The USA and most of the nations were printing money for years, and reduced loan interest rates to promote spending & hence the economy. They were delaying current challenges and problems to later by doing this.

Now the only way to stop inflation is to stop purchasing, if we have less money to spend economy will shrink. Fed plays a major role in this, and now they doing the same, other government’s also closing the tap to pull the excess money resulting in a huge recovery in capital markets.

“At a high level, the market isn’t as challenged as it was in during the .com crash of the global financial crisis, but the story underneath the surface is more revealing when you look beyond the mega caps” - sequoia

The Recovery in the Economy

As a cascading effect of all of these, all economies & capital markets are going through a huge recovery. The growth was driven by FOMO and not by fundamentals. Tech stocks are falling, same for crypto. Even if the fundamentals and balance sheets are good they are falling. We are experiencing the 3rd largest Nasdaq drawdown in 20 years (-28%).

61% of all software, internet and fintech companies are trading below pre-pandemic 2020 prices.

How long the recovery will last? Nobody knows but according to sequoia this won’t be so quick, it’ll take longer, how long? Longer than expected.

“It will be a long recovery and while we can’t predict how long, we can advise you on ways to prepare and get through to the other side, This is not a time to panic. It is a time to pause and reassess” — sequoia

Capital WAS Free

Till now capital was free but now it’s expensive meaning Investors and VC Firms were throwing money at startups like crazy. This is also the reason for “Startup” becoming a buzzword in the last few years. They were playing valuation games meaning they funded a startup based on valuation and not giving a shit about how much money (profit) they’ve made. Customer acquisition costs for some Indian start-ups are more than 500₹ which means they are spending a huge amount on marketing, sales, advertisements, referral programs etc.

This was great for startups because they don’t need to think about how much runway they're left with. The reason being they know they’ll get more money in the next series of funding based on valuation.

Note : Runway is the amount of time you can operate at a loss before running out of money. If you’re burning through $10,000 a month and have $60,000 in the bank, you have six months before you’re in serious trouble.

CTA to Companies

“It is not the strongest of the species that survive, nor the most intelligent, but the one most responsive to change.” — CHARLES DARWIN

It’s time to change. Focus is important. Growth at all costs is no longer rewarded. The era of being rewarded for hypergrowth at any cost is quickly coming to an end - sequoia

Companies won’t get capital if they don’t show the results (Profits). This means the only way to survive is they need to start making money by changing priorities and reducing the burn rate. Now every dollar is more precious than it was; you need to start thinking about the runway and navigate accordingly otherwise you’ll need to fire people and eventually die or get acquired at the valuation which you deserve.

Survival of the Quickest,

“First and foremost, we must recognize the changing environment and shift our mindset to respond with intention rather than regret”

Reduce burn rate → Extend the runway → Figure out ways to become a profitable business → Generate cash

This won’t happen so quickly, but steps toward medium to long term disciplined monetary growth will be rewarded and translates into meaningful appreciation.

The Good thing is that smart companies started adapting;

Unacademy founder Gaurav Munjal wrote an email to his employees titled “Winter is Coming!”

“This is a test for all of us. We must learn to work under constraints and focus on profitability at all costs. We must survive the winter,” said Unacademy’s co-founder and CEO Gaurav Munjal in a letter to the employees”

Unacademy is now thinking about the runway left and navigating accordingly. They will decrease the cash burn on their subsidiaries relevel and Graphy because they aren’t profitable yet and still making the distribution. They have given deadlines to employees to be profitable in all tets prep categories on the platform.

What for Talents?

Leave the unprofitable organization if you do not believe in their vision & founders (If you do believe, then it’s a different case)

Salaries of all engineers Especially developers will drop at the rate at which valuation will drop. If there’s not much cash to spend who will give you that much. the worst part is you can’t auction, negotiate or bargain (if that fits here) because everyone is going through the same. The entire economy will show the change and not the particular set of companies.

If you are one of the high cost to the company, then you might be in trouble even if you are the smartest or most talented, this is how the economy works. This doesn’t mean that your value dropped but you were paid more earlier when they had money.

And as I said you’ll figure some ways out if you are smart and adapts quickly, you will always survive in the arena.

What For the Win?

“Chance only favours the prepared mind” — LOUIS PASTEUR

If the one with all the money is the one that wins, how could Zappos go up against Amazon and win the shoe category? How could DoorDash come from behind and emerge as the market leader beating UberEats? — sequoia

The one who is best prepared for the fight will win. Clubhouse wasn’t prepared, Rest you know.

To Prepare, Sequoia talks about the checklist;

Confront the reality and understand the market conditions, don’t live in the hope. (For this they tell the story of Admiral Jim Stockdale ).

Control your fear : Crisis leads to opportunity if you show courage in tough times.

You cannot overtake 15 cars in sunny weather, but you can when it’s raining.” — AYRTON SENNA

2. Prepare your team

There are two types of people in the organization, Missionaries & Mercenaries. Missionaries are those who believe in your mission and Mercenaries are there only to get shit done and they don’t care about your vision & mission. Keep the core team together. Be fully transparent with them about everything, Share how much runway you left with & discuss how we can do best with the resources we have. As a founder, it’s your job to not let missionaries become mercenaries.

3. Prepare your company

Make sure the company have smooth cash flow and cut down on extra spending. Stop experimental campaigns and focus on what is working now. Take debt if needed even if it’s expensive coz paisa to chahiye na yar!

What’s next according to Sequoia?

The intention is not to be a beacon of gloom. Far from it, we believe the best, most ambitious, most determined of you will use this moment to rise to the occasion and build something truly remarkable. That was true for Cisco after the crash in 1987, Google and PayPal in 2000 after the dot-com bust, Airbnb in 2008 in midst of the financial crisis, and DoorDash in 2020 during the health pandemic.

But we also believe that winning in the years ahead is going to depend on making hard, decisive choices — confronting uncomfortable challenges that may have been masked during the exuberance and distortions of free capital over the past two years.

The primary goal of this session is to shift our collective mindset. We are at a moment of uncertainty and change — a Crucible Moment where your decisions will have a major bearing on the outcome for your company.

Thank you guys for reading the article. I’m just learning to share my thoughts on the internet in the form of articles. Let me know your feedback I’ll really appreciate that.

You can also connect with me on other social media : https://linktr.ee/prathameshdukare

Want to see my work : https://peerlist.io/prathamesh

Items.Title: Crework 体验 | The Crework Experience
Items.Description:
选择过程很简单，你填写申请表解释你想加入的原因，根据你在申请中的回答，他们主要检查两件事；

  1. 你对与 Web 开发相关的东西有很好的理解吗？
  2. 您是否有构建产品以解决问题的心态？

之后你将进行面试，他们将为封闭队列选择 10 名候选人，这将组成两个团队。 该队列的总持续时间为 7 周，第一周是构思阶段，所有成员将就问题集思广益。 最后，根据成员的兴趣，将两个问题分配给两个团队。

接下来的 6 周用于按计划构建问题的解决方案，最后与社区共享产品！

我的经验！

在第一周，我们就问题与导师进行了头脑风暴。 我们最终遇到了两个问题，我决定加入 Noted 团队（我们称其为 sasta 概念，大声笑😀）

每周我们都会与导师开会讨论我们的进步，他们在我们需要帮助的地方帮助我们。 我们还与来宾和我们的导师就不同主题进行了信息交流，例如来自Peerlist的Akash的产品构建、Smarth Vasdev的产品思考、 Manthan & Dushyant的低级设计。

这些都是很棒的学习经历。 我对看产品有了新的看法。 这是我第一次在团队中工作，我学会了如何在项目上进行协作以及如何在团队中做出决定。 我们犯了错误——通过导师的反馈改进它们。 我们使用 Trello 来跟踪分配的任务。 我与队友、导师和其他 crework 成员建立了良好的联系。

构建、交互和学习的体验非常棒。 它教会了我很多无法用几句话表达的东西。 现在，我将我使用的每个应用程序都视为一个产品。 这些我觉得对于一个好的开发者来说是非常重要的。

今天，当我回想起我决定填写 crework 申请的那一天，我觉得这是我在职业生涯早期做出的最好的决定，它教会了我很多我以后可能会学到的东西在我的职业生涯中。

船员工作演示日！船员工作； 随机的人聚在一起，组成团队，为同一个目标工作，并在短短两周内完成了全栈项目。 令人印象深刻的东西！ 喜欢@CreworkHQ 的整体理念和“建设者”的活力。 此外，来自 Ishan、Riten 和 Manthan 的深刻见解。 我再次感谢Ishant和我所有的导师 -
我们构建了什么？

下一篇文章将介绍我们的产品，我将在其中进行解释；

  1. 注意事项
  2. 我们是如何建造它的
  3. 我们面临的挑战
  4. 功能和未来范围

所以敬请期待🌷

我会强烈推荐一个加入 crework 的体验。 这是第一个网络开发批次。 第二批即将推出更多有趣的东西。 如果您想加入 crework，请填写候补名单表格并在社交网站上关注crework以了解最新信息。

这是我对 crework 的体验。 如果您有任何问题/反馈，请告诉我。 您也可以在Twitter 上与我联系。

谢谢😊

原文:

The selection process is simple, you fill out the application form explaining why you want to join and based on your response in the application they’ll check for mainly two things ;

  1. Do you have a good understanding of stuff related to Web Development?
  2. Do you have the mindset to build a product in order to solve a problem?

After that you’ll give an interview, they’ll select 10 candidates for the closed cohort which will form two teams. (this was the case with the first batch)

The total duration of the cohort is 7 weeks, the first week is for the ideation phase in which all the members will brainstorm on problems. Finally, two problems will be assigned to two teams based on the Interests of members.

The next 6 weeks are for building a solution for the problem as planned and finally, the product is shared with the community!

My Experience!

In the first week, we brainstormed with mentors about the problems. We ended up with two problems where I decided to join the team Noted (we were calling it sasta notion, LOL😀)

Every week we had a meeting with mentors about our progress, they helped us where we needed help. We also had Informative sessions with guests and our mentors on different topics such as product building by Akash from Peerlist, product thinking by Smarth Vasdev, Low-Level Design by Manthan & Dushyant.

Prathamesh Dukare✨🗻🏝️ on Twitter: "Insightful session by @designerdada about product design , Got new perspective to look at productThanks @CreworkHQ for arranging this pic.twitter.com/hLWheJdoqg / Twitter"

Insightful session by @designerdada about product design , Got new perspective to look at productThanks @CreworkHQ for arranging this pic.twitter.com/hLWheJdoqg

Prathamesh Dukare✨🗻🏝️ on Twitter: "Great Session by @SmarthVasdev ❄Got new insights to look at a #Product Thanks @CreworkHQ for arranging this🙌🙌 pic.twitter.com/3hm0NGEoN7 / Twitter"

Great Session by @SmarthVasdev ❄Got new insights to look at a #Product Thanks @CreworkHQ for arranging this🙌🙌 pic.twitter.com/3hm0NGEoN7

Prathamesh Dukare✨🗻🏝️ on Twitter: "Great Session by @dusht1408 on DB Schema as a part of Low Level Design (LLD) series.🙌🙌Thanks @manthanguptaa & @dusht1408 & @CreworkHQ 🌟 pic.twitter.com/PYWLTYAAMD / Twitter"

Great Session by @dusht1408 on DB Schema as a part of Low Level Design (LLD) series.🙌🙌Thanks @manthanguptaa & @dusht1408 & @CreworkHQ 🌟 pic.twitter.com/PYWLTYAAMD

Those were great learning experiences. I got the new perceptive on looking at a product. Manthan & Dushyant’s sessions on LLD were very helpful in defining DB schema, and API structure also got to know the importance of OOPs for scalability & performance.

This was the first time I was working in a team, I learnt how to collaborate on projects & how to make decisions in a team. We made mistakes — improved on them with the mentor’s feedback. We used Trello to keep track of tasks assigned. I’ve Made great connections with teammates, mentors & other crework members.

The experience of building, interacting, and learning was awesome. It taught me lots of things which can’t be expressed in just a few words. Now I look at every application I use, as a product. These things I feel are very important for a good developer.

Today, when I look back at the day when I took the decision to fill the application for crework, I feel that it was the best decision I took at the early stage of my career which taught me lots of things which I might have learnt later in my career.

Vaibhav Matere ✨ on Twitter: "CrewWork Demo day!Crew work; where random people came together, formed teams, work on same goal and made full stack projects in just two weeks. Pretty Impressive stuff! Love overall idea of @CreworkHQ and energy of "builders". Also, Great insights from Ishan, Riten & Manthan. pic.twitter.com/q5bxHGo0Wi / Twitter"

CrewWork Demo day!Crew work; where random people came together, formed teams, work on same goal and made full stack projects in just two weeks. Pretty Impressive stuff! Love overall idea of @CreworkHQ and energy of "builders". Also, Great insights from Ishan, Riten & Manthan. pic.twitter.com/q5bxHGo0Wi

Again I’m thankful to Ishant & all my mentors -
Sahil, Manthan, Dushyant, Aniket, Akshat, Ruchika etc for building crework.

Prathamesh Dukare on LinkedIn: #crework #cohort #webdev #2022batch

What we’ve Build?

Noted : A note taking/productivity tool for taking notes while watching YouTube videos. Next article will be on our product in which I’ll be explaining;

  1. What is Noted
  2. How we have built it
  3. Challenges we faced
  4. Features & Future scope

So stay tuned 🌷

I’ll highly recommend one joining crework for the experience. This was the first web dev batch. The second batch is coming soon with more interesting stuff. If you want to join crework fill out the waitlist form & follow crework on socials to stay updated.

This was my experience with crework. Let me know if you have questions/feedback. You can also connect with me on Twitter.

Thank you 😊

Items.Description:
你好真棒人👋，

这些天我正在阅读 Yuval Noah Harari 所著的《智人》一书，其中我遇到了一个关于人和社区如何运作的非常有趣的概念。 它们的形成是因为具有相同心态、目标和观念的人们为了分享经验、知识和生活中发生的所有好事/坏事而聚在一起。 它植根于存在于人们集体想象中的常见神话。 但有一件事是肯定的，有时你需要有人让你感觉/意识到，“你属于我们”，我认为Akash和Yogini在Peerlist做得很好。

他们正在为技术人员围绕 Peerlist 产品建立一个很棒的社区。

宇宙中没有神，没有国家，没有金钱，没有人权，没有人类共同观念之外的法律和正义——Yuval Noah Harari，智人

不明白？ 等等，让我解释一下……

求职者用一堆无用的东西写基本简历的日子已经一去不复返了。 如今，在公司担任相同职位的竞争非常激烈。 所以，抓住机会并不容易。 您需要脱颖而出，才能被潜在的招聘人员看到。 为此，您需要提供 Signal。 为了发出信号，如今人们正在做传统做法之外的其他事情。 他们通过撰写文章/时事通讯、在 product hunt 上构建和发布产品、在 youtube 上创建内容、制作出色的设计并在 Figma/Dribble 社区上分享、在 GitHub 上为开源项目做贡献以及做一大堆来分享他们的学习和经验其他事情。

因此，如果您不在各种平台上策划您的所有工作、很好地展示它并与能够欣赏您的工作的人分享，那么单独完成所有这些并没有多大用处。 这就是Peerlist 发挥作用的地方。

得益于无缝的 Peerlist 集成，它允许您跨平台展示您的作品。 这是您可以拥有的最有意义的工作档案，您可以在其中展示您的技能、工作经验、项目以及我们上面讨论的所有内容。 好处是创建和管理您的同行列表资料非常简单且轻松。 您可以重新排序您的个人资料内容，以突出您的最佳作品。

除此之外，还有很多你可以做的......

在 peerlist.io 上探索更多

整个世界都建立在信任之上。 但由于竞争，候选人通过在简历/linkedin 中添加虚拟经验、技能等来美化事物，并试图引起注意。 我在周围看到了很多。 如今很难获得信任。 招聘人员也无法在不经历整个面试过程的情况下判断合适的候选人，我们知道这是公司最耗时和资源的任务。

您不需要做太多事情，Peerlist 会处理好这件事。 现在你在工作经验面前有了一个经过验证的标签，因此你获得了信任。

当我写“我非常了解 Typescript”时，除了说我了解 TypeScript 之外，我还需要一些能为我提倡的东西。 这就是工作证明。 而不是说； 告诉他们，告诉他们！ 你肯定会得到一些加分。

不要错过同行名单工作的最佳机会。 检查一下：https://peerlist.io/jobs

  1. 对于个人：https://peerlist.io/individuals

  2. 对于招聘人员：https://peerlist.io/recruiters

我的同行名单故事

我的 Peerlist 故事真的很吸引人。 我正在浏览 Peerlist滚动（很明显！🙂）并偶然发现了Salman的帖子； 发现这个帖子很有趣，所以我出于好奇对他的帖子发表了评论。 从那时起，Salman 和我开始就科技领域的事物进行良好的对话。

现在，我们是一起建造和破坏东西的好朋友。 这不就是 Peerlist 的目的之一吗？ 网络🙌。

我看到 Peerlist 从无到有地成长起来，并且真正相信产品及其与社区一起解决的问题。 很快，Peerlist 将改变很多关于专业网络、工作证明和招聘的事情。 还有很多事情要做。

社区每天都在滚雪球，帮助个人与志趣相投的人建立联系并获得良好的机会。

很快在社区中， 与招聘人员分享 Peerlist 个人资料而不是简历将成为技术招聘中的常态，尤其是在创业领域。

在这里，我试图模仿上下文。 感谢您阅读本文直到最后。 与您的朋友分享，不要忘记查看我的 peerlist 个人资料。 如果您还没有，请在几分钟内创建您的个人资料，让我们成为同行。

我刚刚开始学习以文章的形式在互联网上分享我的想法，所以如果您能在下面的评论中写下您的反馈和想法，那就太好了。

My Peerlist story

Akash also commented on the post in order to understand more about the context. From Then, Salman and I started having good conversations about things in tech.

Now, we are good friends building and breaking stuff together. Isn't this one of the purposes of Peerlist? Networking 🙌.

I have seen Peerlist growing from scratch and really believing in the product and the problems it is solving with the community. Soon, Peerlist will change lots of things regarding professional networking, proof of work and hiring. Lot more things to come.

Peerlist has a very good UBP - unique bragworthy proposition which helps in the organic growth of the platform. The community is snowballing day by day, helping individuals network with like-minded folks and get good opportunities.

Soon in the community, sharing Peerlist profile to recruiters instead of resumes will become a usual thing in tech hiring, especially in the startup world.

Items.Title: Steam 甲板上的自定义 Css 加载器主题 | Custom Css loader themes on steam deck
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我将介绍如何在 Steam 甲板 UI 上创建自定义 CSS 元素。 您需要安装 Decky-loader 才能启动，您可以在github找到说明。 如果您还不熟悉，现在首先让我们了解什么是 css loader。

什么是 css 加载器

如果你还没有通过 Decky-loader 安装 css-loader 现在是时候了。

创建自定义主题

我强烈建议使用此模板存储库开始搞乱。

创建自定义主题时，文件结构落在 theme.json 和 shared.css 文件上，示例如下

//theme.json { "name": 'menu color theme' "author": "Me", "manifest_version": 1, "target": "Other", "description": 'changes steam menu color' "inject": { "shared.css": ["MainMenu"] } } //shared.css // \/ the class name. .mainmenu_Menu_23IDi { background: #36146e}

获取steam deck ui类名的方法是通过CEF调试器。

如何使用 CEF 调试器

在 Decky-loader 常规设置中启用“允许远程 CEF 调试”

连接到您的 Steam 平台

通过转到互联网设置并单击已连接的网络来获取您的 Steam 平台 IP

在浏览器中获取后，转到 {browser}://inspect（chrome://inspect、brave://inspect、arc://inspect）

单击 Discover network targets 上的 configure 并插入 {steamdeck ip}:8081，完成后应该会出现一个新的远程目标，并像这样连接你的甲板 ip。

  1. 了解调试器现在已经连接，我们可以访问主要的 UI 选项卡，请记住，要正确访问选项卡，您的 steamdeck 必须打开并查看所述选项卡。

  • 主菜单
    此选项卡代表“蒸汽”按钮菜单

  • 快速访问
    快速访问菜单是“...”按钮菜单

要了解如何进行实时编辑，我将通过一个更改 MainMenu 颜色样式的示例来了解样式。

首先，我需要检查 MainMenu 选项卡并确保 steamdeck 可以看到该选项卡。
最好使用选择元素按钮来查找和更改特定的 css 元素。

我使用 select 元素获取主菜单的主 div 和类名，并通过样式调试器过滤类名。

现在我所要做的就是将背景样式更改为我选择的颜色。

这是查看和设置 Steam 甲板 UI 样式的主要过程。 要更好地了解该过程，请访问CSS 加载器 themeDB 存储库以获取更多信息

结论

我希望这篇开发文章能让你开始创建自己的自定义样式变得更容易一些，再次不要害怕使用模板存储库和官方 css 加载器 themDB 存储库来最大程度地扩展你对这项新技术的了解。
感谢您的阅读！



原文:

In this article I will going over how to create custom CSS elements on the steam deck UI. You will need to have Decky-loader installed to start, you can find the instructions at there github. Now firstly lets understand what css loader is if your not already familiar.

what is css loader

Css Loader is a npm module mostly used for web-pack that interpretes @import and url()'s to import/require, but when it comes to the Decky-loader, it is a plugin that allows for the download of custom css elements like curved borders, custom backgrounds, color changes, and more. If you have not installed css-loader through Decky-loader now is the time.

Creating custom themes

I highly recommend using this template repository to begin messing around.

When creating a custom theme the file structure falls upon a theme.json and shared.css file, example below

//theme.json
{
  "name": 'menu color theme'
  "author": "Me",
    "manifest_version": 1,
    "target": "Other",
    "description": 'changes steam menu color'
    "inject": { "shared.css": ["MainMenu"] }
}

//shared.css
// \/ the class name.
.mainmenu_Menu_23IDi { background: #36146e}

The shared.css file will use the class names to add on or edit the way it appears, the theme.json file will determine where everything will be displayed and apply options (if any) to the theme. The method to obtain the class names of the steam deck ui is through the CEF debugger.

How to use the CEF Debugger

The CEF Debugger allows you to directly mess around with the styling of the steam deck UI, here is the steps to enable and start working with it.

In the Decky-loader general settings enable "Allow Remote CEF Debugging"

(optional) You can also enable auto-enable on the CEF remote debugger in the steam deck developer setting in miscellaneous

  1. Connect to your steam deck

Obtain your steam deck IP by going to internet settings and clicking on the connected network

once obtained in a browser go to {browser}://inspect (chrome://inspect, brave://inspect, arc://inspect)

click configure on Discover network targets and insert {steamdeck ip}:8081, once done a new remote target should appear with your decks ip attached like so.

  1. Understanding the debugger Now that were connected we have access to the main UI tabs, keep in mind that to access the tabs correctly your steamdeck must be on and viewing said tab.

  • SP
    SP is the steam decks main user interface to the current page being viewed, this includes but is not limited to home, library, settings, and store

  • MainMenu
    This tab represents the 'steam' button menu

  • QuickAccess
    The quick access menu is the '...' button menu

To understand how to make live edits the styling im going to go through an example of changing the color styles of the MainMenu.

To start I will need to inspect the MainMenu tab and make sure the steamdeck has the tab in view.
Its best to use the select element button to find and make changes to specific css elements.

I used the select element to get the main div and class name of the main menu and filterd the class name through the styling debugger.

now all i have to do is change the background style to my selected color.

This is the main process to viewing and styling the steam deck UI. The shared.css and theme.json files are used to upload the theme to the css loader plugin in decky. To get a better understanding of that process, please go to the CSS loader themeDB repository for more information

conclusion

I hope this dev post made it a bit easier to begin creating your own custom styling, once again do not be afraid to use the template repository and official css loader themDB repository to expand your knowledge of this new technology to the fullest.
Thank you for reading!
